model_narrative: We solve an addition puzzle in which several words are spelled out with letters and the letters stand for digits. We must assign a digit to every letter so that the digits spell out a correct addition, where two numbers added to a third give a fourth. Every letter takes a different digit, and a leading letter cannot be zero. Reading the addition column by column from right to left produces a carry out of each column into the next. We decide which digit each letter receives, the resulting numeric value of each letter, and the carry out of each column. The objective is to minimize the total of all the carries.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of distinct letters that appear in the puzzle; each letter must be assigned a digit

sets.name: j
sets.doc: a second copy of the letter set used as the column index when assigning digits to letters; it is an alias of the letter set

sets.name: k
sets.doc: the addition columns of the puzzle in order from the rightmost column to the leftmost; the first member is the units column and each later member is the next column to the left

sets.name: lead
sets.doc: the subset of letters that appear as the leading letter of a word; a leading letter is not allowed to take the digit zero

params.name: lhs
params.doc: how many times each letter appears in a given column on the left-hand side of the addition, that is among the numbers being added together; zero when the letter does not appear in that column

params.name: rhs
params.doc: how many times each letter appears in a given column on the right-hand side of the addition, that is in the result number; zero when the letter does not appear in that column

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the digit-assignment indicator, equal to 1 when a letter is assigned the digit corresponding to a given column position and 0 otherwise; the second index ranges over the same labels as the letters but its ordinal position from the start, counting from zero, is the digit value it stands for

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the numeric digit value assigned to each letter, ranging from zero to nine

vars.name: c
vars.doc: the carry produced out of each column and passed into the next column to the left

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total of all the carries, which is the quantity being minimized

constraints.name: k)
constraints.intent: The quantity being minimized is the running total of carries. Set the total to the sum, taken over the columns, of the carry that comes into each column from the column to its right. The rightmost column has nothing coming into it, so it contributes nothing to this total.

constraints.name: k)
constraints.intent: Each column of the addition must balance once carries are taken into account. For every column, take the carry coming in from the column to its right and add to it the combined value of the letters being added together in that column, where each letter contributes its assigned value as many times as it appears there. This must equal the combined value of the letters that make up the result in that column, again counting each letter's value by how many times it appears, plus ten times the carry that this column sends out to the next column. The leftmost column sends out no carry, so for that column there is no carry-out term.

constraints.name: y[i]
constraints.intent: Each letter's numeric value is determined by which digit it was assigned. For every letter, set its value equal to the digit it was given under the digit assignment.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every letter must receive exactly one digit. For each letter, its assignment across all the possible digits must add up to one.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every digit must be given to exactly one letter, so that no two letters share a digit. For each digit, its assignment across all the letters must add up to one.

constraints.name: y[lead]
constraints.intent: A leading letter is not allowed to stand for zero. For each leading letter, its assigned value must be at least one.

query: We're curious whether the two big lead letters G and M have to be neighbours on the digit line. If we add the condition that M's digit comes out exactly one above G's, what does the optimizer return for the carry sum?

query: Let's look at the case where we pin the letter I to the zero digit explicitly by switching on that assignment cell. Does the equation still balance, and what's the resulting objective?

query: Suppose a downstream check needs the digits behind G and V to sum to nine. We're looking at adding that as a side condition — what carry total does the model come back with when it's enforced?

query: N and A show up together in a lot of columns, so it feels like N ought to be at least as big as A. Why isn't the solver just locking that ordering in? Make N's digit be no smaller than A's and tell me what gives.

query: R and E sit side by side in the same words, so surely R should come out exactly one digit above E. Couldn't we just pin that one-step gap and be done? What's keeping the model from treating that as obvious?
